sections.paragraph:
ἐνεργής, ές (s. ἐνέργεια, ἐνεργέω; Aristot. et al.; Polyb. 11, 23, 2; Plut., Sol. 96 [31, 2] v.l.; Diosc., Mat. Med. 1, 18; Vett. Val. 276, 11; Herm. Wr. 10, 23; POxy 1088, 56 [1 a.d.]; Just.) pert. to practical expression of capability, effective, active, powerful, κοινωνία τῆς πίστεως Phlm 6. ὁ λόγος τοῦ θεοῦ Hb 4:12. Of a door (fig.), the opening of which promises a rich field of labor 1 Cor 16:9.—M-M. TW.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 335
